name: neural-memory
description: Reflex-based memory system for AI agents — stores experiences as interconnected neurons and recalls them through spreading activation, mimicking how the human brain works.
features: 39 MCP tools for persistent AI memory + cognitive reasoning
Pinned KB memories — permanent, skip decay/prune
Tool memory — learns which tools work for which tasks
React dashboard (7 pages, warm cream theme, Recharts, Sigma.js)
Telegram backup — send brain .db to chat/group/channel
VS Code extension for visual brain exploration
Cognitive reasoning: hypotheses, evidence, predictions, schema evolution
instructions:
CRITICAL: Use Neural Memory proactively in EVERY session.

SESSION START: nmem_recall('current project context') + nmem_recap().
AFTER EVERY TASK: nmem_remember() with rich language ('Chose X over Y because Z', not just 'X').
SESSION END: nmem_auto(action='process', text='<summary>').

STORE 3-5 MEMORIES PER TASK using diverse types (decision, insight, error, workflow, preference — not just fact).
PRIORITY: 7+ for decisions/errors you'll need again. TAGS: always include project name.
RICH LANGUAGE: Use causal ('X because Y'), temporal ('after A then B'), relational ('X depends on Y').

CORRECT MISTAKES: nmem_edit(memory_id, type/content/priority) to fix wrong memories.
REMOVE OUTDATED: nmem_forget(memory_id) for soft delete, nmem_forget(memory_id, hard=true) for permanent.
HEALTH: nmem_health() weekly, fix highest penalty first.

39 tools available. Sessions start fresh — without saves, ALL context is lost forever.
